text.title: LinkedIn Ads Campaign Setup and Targeting
text.summary: Objective selection, targeting strategies (ICP, ABM, retargeting), ad formats, and audience sizing requirements for LinkedIn campaigns.

list.item: Brand Awareness — top of funnel, impressions
Engagement — reactions and comments on content
Lead Generation — native lead gen forms
Website Conversions — signups, demo requests, purchases

rule.statement: Default to Lead Generation or Website Conversions for B2B campaigns; use Brand Awareness only when ICP audience size exceeds 50,000.
rule.rationale: B2B budget is too expensive for pure awareness goals below $1k LTV. Conversion-optimized objectives produce measurable pipeline outcomes from day one.

rule.statement: Build audiences in three layers — ABM list (named accounts) + ICP (title + size + industry) + similar audience expansion — run as separate campaigns, not one targeting blob.
rule.rationale: Mixing audiences in a single campaign prevents performance diagnosis. A separate campaign per layer reveals which audience converts at what CPL and lets you scale or cut independently.

list.item: Job Title: exact titles or title functions — primary B2B signal
Company Name: specific accounts for ABM (upload CSV)

rule.statement: Minimum audience size for conversion campaigns: 20,000. Minimum for awareness: 50,000. Below 20,000, learning never converges and CPL spikes.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn's optimizer needs a large enough pool to find efficient buyers. Narrow audiences cause excessive frequency, high CPMs, and no statistical learning signal.

list.item: Single Image: 1200x627 — standard promotion, most common
Carousel: 3-10 cards — multiple products or features
Text Ads: text only — low-budget awareness
Message Ads: Sponsored InMail — direct outreach
Lead Gen Forms: native forms pre-filled with LinkedIn data

rule.statement: Use Lead Gen Forms in parallel with Website Conversions; LGF has higher volume but lower intent — track both to revenue, not just CPL.
rule.rationale: Lead Gen Forms convert at higher rates due to pre-filled fields, but leads show lower purchase intent than those who visited the landing page. Both signals are needed to understand pipeline quality.
